excerpt: During this blessed month, the apportionment of sustenance (rizq) and the determination of life-spans are established... The entire year may pass in the ordinary course of things, yet the allocation of sustenance and the decreeing of destinies occur uniquely on the Night of Power.

All the channels (munāfiẓ) that customarily open at different times throughout the other months are opened simultaneously during the days consecrated to God. On the Day of God's City, emanation (ishrāq) issues forth from all eighteen channels together.

When the city of Ramaḍān arrived, Maulānā ʿAlī (upon him be peace) would say to his children: 'Exert yourselves with your souls, for in this month is apportioned the sustenance of all, the terms of all lifespans are fixed, and recorded are those of God's delegation who are prepared to proceed unto Him.'
source_authority: Sayings of Imām ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib

excerpt: The Scribe (kitāb) signifies Imām al-Zamān (upon him be peace). He determines which souls shall and shall not ascend to his temple. It is as a father who arranges the betterment of his son in the outward life—and all such matters are enacted by the obedience to Imām al-Zamān.
Imām Jaʿfar as-Ṣādiq declared: 'Allāh possesses the authority to originate (ibdāʾ) and to decree (mashīʾa) all things save the Imamate.'
source_authority: Kirmani's philosophical treatise on intellect and Imamate

excerpt: In other months, whoever causes a believer to break his fast receives forgiveness of sins. Yet especially in the city of Allāh, the reward of the one who hosts the breaking of the fast is doubled, and one receives recompense as though one had hosted the ifṭār in the first age.
At that time, he was blessed with the divine secrets, which have been expounded by Sayedna Hibatullāh in his compendium Fayz al-Nūr al-Sārī wa Sirr al-Nūrānī.

The Messenger of Allāh (upon him be blessings and peace) once declared: 'O people, Gabriel (upon him be peace) has just come to me and extended his greeting... Whoever finds the month of Ramaḍān and is not forgiven and dies, let him be cast into Hell—and may Allāh remove him.'

If he reaches the field of ʿArafāt—that is, if his dimension is revealed during the age of disclosure and is not imprisoned—he shall be forgiven. He shall not be imprisoned there. This occurs when the wise one allows his virtues to appear in the distant revelation, such that no retributions remain upon him.
The Tahajjud prayer (night vigil) is connected to the true prayer, and this can only be performed by one who receives the Holy Spirit from his Imām (upon him be peace).
source_authority: Philosophical treatise on knowledge and hierarchy
source_location_hint: Section 9, Three things pleasing to God—Vigil of Night
